title: 'Abhishek is like my son': Kalyan Banerjee softens tone after calling TMC leader 'arrogant'
content: TMC leader Kalyan Banerjee softened his stance on Abhishek Banerjee, calling him 'like his son' after recent criticism. This comes amid a significant internal party crisis with several MPs seeking separate recognition. Banerjee also dismissed the threat posed by rebel MPs, accusing them of aligning with the BJP and facing harassment.

title: Delhiites to pay more for power from next month
content: Delhi power bills are set to increase for lakhs of consumers as the DERC allows discoms to recover higher fuel and power purchase costs. Consumers in BYPL areas will see a 5.7% rise, while BRPL consumers face a 3.4% increase, effective from July. This hike follows a sharp rise in procurement costs due to increased demand and expensive market purchases.

title: Bihar residents waited 12 years for Rs 26-crore overbridge. It shuts within week due to damage
content: Traffic on the Buxar–Baruna rail overbridge has been suspended after a slab on a pillar was damaged, exacerbating the woes of residents. With the bridge and the adjacent railway crossing now closed, commuters face severe inconvenience and long delays, as authorities scramble for temporary solutions.

title: After AI talent war, Zuckerberg says AI progress doesn't need hundreds of researchers
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg believes significant AI progress can be achieved by small, highly capable teams, despite the ongoing talent war. He highlighted that mission-driven work, like that at Biohub, can attract researchers seeking unique challenges beyond traditional AI labs. Zuckerberg also noted AI's potential to accelerate scientific research, particularly in healthcare, while acknowledging compute resource limitations.

title: 'Please give me one chance': Pranit More apologises over viral Rs 370 biryani remark
content: Comedian Pranit More has apologized for his 'Rs 370 biryani' remark controversy, admitting a lapse in judgment for not intervening during a show where derogatory comments were made. He expressed remorse for offending people and is cooperating with authorities investigating the viral clips for alleged obscenity and violations of public decency.

title: 'Same land that produced Aurangzeb also produced people like him': Sanjay Raut's swipe at PM
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Sanjay Raut has drawn a controversial comparison between Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Mughal emperor Aurangzeb. This statement comes amid intense speculation about the future of the Uddhav Thackeray-led party within the opposition. Sources suggest potential defections from Shiv Sena (UBT) to the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena are being closely watched.

title: 'Lose to anyone, but not Pakistan': Jemimah ahead of IND vs PAK opener
content: Ahead of India’s Women’s T20 World Cup opener against Pakistan, Jemimah Rodrigues said the rivalry brings immense pressure, recalling how even her building watchman stresses the importance of beating Pakistan. Despite the expectations, she said the team remains focused on winning its maiden T20 World Cup title, with extensive preparation and mental conditioning boosting confidence.

title: Home to Bezos & Zuckerberg, Florida battles 4-foot-long invasive lizards
content: Wildlife officials in several US states are intensifying efforts to curb the spread of the Argentine black-and-white tegu, an invasive lizard. With the species' main hatching season in June and July, concerns are rising about its impact on native wildlife, farms, and ecosystems as populations expand beyond Florida.

title: Inside An-32: Aircraft that became backbone of India's remote frontiers
content: India's Air Force relies heavily on the robust Antonov-32, a workhorse since 1984, excelling in hot and high conditions for crucial Himalayan missions. Despite a recent $400 million upgrade, the aircraft has faced tragic incidents, underscoring operational risks. As newer planes arrive, the An-32's legacy as a vital transport asset endures.

title: From Lebanon ceasefire to nuclear talks - what Iran proposed in 'Islamabad Agreement'
content: Iran has outlined conditions for a potential US agreement, prioritizing sanctions relief and maritime access over immediate nuclear program discussions. Tehran insists on lifting the US naval blockade and a new arrangement for the Strait of Hormuz, while also seeking to include Lebanon in the deal.

title: Prarthana Behere hails Dhurandhar for fresh cinema; says not to judge such films like critics
content: Prarthana Behere is thrilled about her role in the upcoming film 'Mardini', a heartfelt exploration of motherhood with the support of Shreyas Talpade. She feels a shift in Indian cinema towards more profound storytelling, highlighting 'Dhurandhar' as a prime example.

title: As US bans Fable 5 and Mythos 5, Anthropic shares a 700-plus word statement
content: The US government has ordered Anthropic to suspend access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models due to national security concerns. Anthropic disagrees, stating the identified "jailbreak" technique exposed minor, known vulnerabilities also present in other leading AI systems. The company is complying with the directive but argues it's an overreaction to a narrow issue.

title: NEET UG paper leak accused seeking to write re-exam, denied bail plea hearing
content: A Delhi court deferred hearing on Yash Yadav's interim bail plea for the NEET UG exam, deeming it premature as he lacks an admit card. The court will revisit the matter on June 16, after seeking a response from the CBI regarding Yadav's request for bail to attend his sister's wedding and the examination.

title: Indus Waters Treaty fallout? Nearly a third of Pakistan faces water crisis
content: Pakistan grapples with a severe water shortage affecting one-third of its population. Last year, India suspended the Indus Waters Treaty after terror attacks. This has intensified water scarcity in Sindh and Balochistan. Irrigation canals face significant deficits. Allegations of unequal water distribution fuel political disputes. Farmers struggle as cultivation is hampered. The crisis highlights vulnerabilities in Pakistan's water management.

title: Lionel Messi event row: Bengal police issue third notice to TMC leader Arup Biswas
content: TMC leader and former minister Arup Biswas has received a third notice from Bidhannagar commissionerate regarding the Lionel Messi "GOAT India Tour" event. He is directed to appear before Bidhannagar South Police Station within 48 hours as part of an ongoing investigation into alleged ticket black marketing, extortion, and fraud.

title: KCET counselling 2026: Web option entry likely to begin today- see how to fill choices
content: The Karnataka Examinations Authority (KEA) is set to commence the KCET 2026 counselling web option entry today, June 13. This crucial stage allows candidates to prioritize their preferred colleges and courses for admission to professional programs. The process follows the release of the KCET 2026 verification slip, confirming document verification.

title: Elon Musk is now world’s first trillionaire - but will his net worth sustain?
content: It’s important to note that a big portion of Musk’s net worth is tied to SpaceX. His holding in the company is worth around $866 billion. According to Reuters calculations, Musk’s net worth adds up to over a trillion dollars after the listing of Space X.

title: USA vs Paraguay: USMNT ends 20-year wait with historic World Cup opener
content: USA's FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign got off to a historic start as the hosts' early breakthrough against Paraguay became the third-fastest goal ever scored by a host nation in its opening World Cup match. The seventh-minute strike helped launch a dominant 4-1 victory, with Mauricio Pochettino's side also showcasing their attacking evolution by surpassing their entire goal tally from the 2022 tournament in a single game.

title: Doddanna denies death rumours: Actor says he is safe and healthy; AI hoax angers fans
content: Veteran Kannada actor Doddanna has debunked a viral social media rumor claiming his death. An AI-generated poster falsely announced his demise on June 12, causing widespread confusion. Doddanna, who was at home watching television, personally clarified that he is alive and well, urging the public to disregard the misinformation. The incident has drawn strong criticism for its insensitivity.

title: 'Drishyam 3' OTT release confirmed: When and where to stream
content: Mohanlal's 'Drishyam 3' is set for its digital premiere on OTT on June 18, following a successful theatrical run. The crime thriller, directed by Jeethu Joseph, has garnered significant box office success, nearing Rs 237 crore worldwide.

title: IND vs AFG: Virat, Hardik injuries open doors for Ishan Kishan and Nitish Reddy
content: Virat Kohli and Hardik Pandya's injuries have forced India into transition mode as they begin preparations for the 2027 ODI World Cup. Ishan Kishan appears set to get the No. 3 role, Nitish Kumar Reddy could replace Hardik, while young pacers Prince and Gurnoor await opportunities.

title: Pranit More returns to social media, says he deserves the hate
content: Stand-up comedian Pranit More has broken his silence over the 'Rs 370 biryani' controversy involving Himanshu Jangra from Gurugram. Admitting his mistake, More said he got carried away as the audience laughed and apologised to those hurt. Maharashtra Cyber Police has filed an FIR against More and others.

title: One year after Air India Ahmedabad crash, no report released
content: The London-bound AI171 crashed immediately after taking off from Ahmedabad airport on June 12, 2025. Of the 242 people on board, 241 were killed, and 19 other victims were from the ground at the hostel complex of Ahmedabad’s B J Medical College that the plane crashed into. The closest international parallel to AI171 may be Ethiopian Airlines Flight 302, the 2019 Boeing 737 MAX crash that killed 157 people.

title: PM Modi in Nice: India and France seek to strengthen long-standing strategic partnership
content: India and France are deepening their strategic partnership, evident in a renewed 10-year defence pact. Recent agreements include reciprocal officer deployments, technology transfer for naval Rafale jets, and expanded industrial cooperation with Safran. Joint military exercises like Shakti, Garuda, and Varuna underscore robust operational ties, extending to space and the Indo-Pacific.

title: 'Irreparable loss': Manu Bhaker shares heartfelt tribute to Jaspal Rana
content: Double Olympic medallist Manu Bhaker expressed profound grief over the passing of her former coach Jaspal Rana, calling it an "irreparable loss." She fondly remembered Rana as a mentor and friend who understood her deeply, crediting him for her resurgence and historic Paris Olympics success. Their reunited partnership brought immense glory to Indian sports.

title: ED breaks locks to enter Abhishek Banerjee's residence during overnight raid
content: A joint police team, including central forces, conducted an overnight search at Trinamool leader Abhishek Banerjee's Kolkata residence. The operation, linked to a Paschim Medinipur case, involved breaking locks after no response from occupants. Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee rushed to the scene amidst the ensuing political controversy.

title: Watch: US 'executes' Venezuela's 'bloodthirsty' prison gang leader Niño Guerrero in strike
content: US President Donald Trump announced a US military strike, conducted at his direction, killed Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, known as Niño Guerrero, leader of the Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua. Trump stated the operation was coordinated with Venezuelan authorities, linking it to his anti-crime and immigration agenda.  The gang is designated a foreign terrorist organization by Washington.

title: FIFA World Cup: Pulisic overtakes Ronaldo in assists as USA rout Paraguay
content: Christian Pulisic became the United States' all-time World Cup assist leader, surpassing Cristiano Ronaldo with his third career assist during a dominant 4-1 victory over Paraguay.  Despite a precautionary halftime substitution due to a calf issue, Pulisic's early contributions set the tone for a historic performance by the USMNT on home soil.

title: UPSC prelims result 2026: The hidden cost of chasing India's toughest exam
content: UPSC Civil Services exam results are out, highlighting the intense competition and low selection rates. Many aspirants dedicate years to preparation, facing emotional and financial costs. Experts advise quitting if performance stagnates or well-being suffers, emphasizing transferable skills gained. Success can also lead to alternative paths, as seen with former civil servants.

title: 1,569 days and counting: Russia-Ukraine war now longer than World War I
content: The Russia-Ukraine war has surpassed World War I in duration, now exceeding 1,569 days. What began as a swift Russian operation has become Europe's longest and bloodiest conflict since WWII, defying early predictions. Despite diplomatic efforts and Western support for Ukraine, a durable peace remains elusive, with no clear end in sight.

title: 'This one's for me': Michael Chandler on UFC Freedom 250, fighting at the White House
content: For much of his career, Michael Chandler chased championships and marquee names. Long before he arrived in the UFC and established himself as one of the lightweight division's biggest attractions, Michael Chandler had already built a reputation as a three-time Bellator champion. At 40, standing on the cusp of a fight unlike any other in MMA history, the American believes he has found something equally valuable: personal validation.

title: Man arrested for 'threatening' to kill Kerala CM Satheesan, family
content: A man identified as Sony Thomas was arrested in Thiruvananthapuram for allegedly making threatening phone calls to kill Kerala Chief Minister V D Satheesan and his family. The accused reportedly made two calls to the police command center, stating he disliked the Chief Minister. Police apprehended Thomas from Attingal, suspecting he was under the influence of alcohol during the calls.

title: 'Won't let Indus waters reach patrons of terror': Rajnath's strong message to Pakistan
content: Defence minister Rajnath Singh declared India will not allow Indus river waters to reach "patrons of terror," reinforcing a firm stance against Pakistan. He highlighted Operation Sindoor as proof of India's resolve and criticized opposition parties. Singh also detailed India's global role, defence sector growth, and the NDA government's achievements, contrasting them with past corruption.

title: 'I am greater than death': How Jaspal Rana gave life to Tagore's 'Mrityunjay'
content: More than a decorated shooter and coach, Jaspal Rana's enduring legacy lay in his generosity and unwavering commitment to nurturing Indian shooting. Through anecdotes from those closest to him and the grief his passing left behind, this piece explores how Rana's life came to embody the spirit of Rabindranath Tagore's Mrityunjay, becoming, in essence, "greater than death".

title: Police serve fresh summons to Bengal's ex-sports minister in Messi event chaos case
content: The case stems from an FIR lodged against him by the event organiser Shatadru Dutta, accusing the former Minister of black-marketing tickets, extortion, criminal intimidation and cheating in connection with the high-profile football event in December, 2005

title: President Murmu reviews IMA passing out parade, hails induction of women cadets as historic milestone
content: 515 officer cadets commissioned, including 34 from 16 friendly nations; President calls for adaptive and ethical military leadership

title: Bihar encounter: Man killed, two police personnel injured
content: “The injured policemen are undergoing treatment at Patna AIIMS, and their condition was stated to be out of danger,” an officer said

title: FIFA 2026: Kerala startup designs football-inspired fonts
content: Fontspell, a startup based in Kozhikode, launches fonts that capture the energy, culture and spirit of football

title: Chellanam, a village near Kochi, celebrates Messi with 25-foot cutout ahead of the FIFA World Cup
content: Placing the cutout of Messi at Chellanam has become something of a World Cup tradition in the coastal village near Kochi

title: BKC Public Transport Day: Tepid response as commuters ask for efficient last mile connectivity
content: The buses and autos are the only modes of transport that provide last-mile connectivity, but it becomes a hassle due to traffic congestion and the high fares of autorickshaws

title: MAATI helps women artists from Bihar to look beyond art
content: MAATI, short for Mithila Art Artisan Transformative Initiative, works with women artisans from Madhubhani and Darbhanga in Bihar. Four women artisans showcased their works in Bengaluru, ranging from textile to wall art

title: Two nursing students killed in road accident
content: The deceased have been identified as Atul and Gaurishankar, both nursing students pursuing their studies in Bengaluru

title: Monsoon 2026 LIVE: Centre suspends Cell Broadcasting Services, a mobile-based disaster warning system
content: Cell Broadcast Service is being kept on hold until further instructions due to certain issues flagged by the competent authorities, reads an order by National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA)

title: Centre suspends Cell Broadcasting Services
content: An order issued by the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) on June 12 stated that, due to certain issues flagged by the competent authorities, the Cell Broadcast Service is being kept on hold until further instructions

title: Friend of deceased arrested in medical college death case in Tripura
content: An official communiqué said West Tripura District Magistrate would conduct the inquiry and submit a report to the State government

title: HC denies bail to 3 juveniles in sexual assault case, notes blind victim identified them by voice
content: The case came to light after the chairman of the Karnal Child Welfare Committee filed a complaint stating he had visited Ansal Town for work, where he saw a girl child who appeared to be pregnant

title: DRDO demonstrates advanced missile defence, anti-ship strike capabilities in three successful flight tests
content: It further mentioned that the multi-layered BMD capability was successfully demonstrated with interceptor missiles engaging and destroying their designated targets.

title: Rahul Gandhi’s approach helping BJP, not strengthening INDIA bloc, says Kerala ex-CM Pinarayi Vijayan
content: Rahul Gandhi had said during a recent INDIA bloc meeting that he would not hug the ex-Chief Minister of Kerala because he had an ongoing political fight with him

title: Mob attacks police outpost in Shahjahanpur, claims rape accused is ‘innocent’; three held
content: A mob attacked a police outpost in Shahjahanpur in protest of the arrest of Guddu Sharma for allegedly raping a child

title: Watch: Now, commercial consumers can’t buy petrol, diesel from regular fuel stations | Explained
content: In an order issued on June 11, the government barred industrial and commercial consumers from purchasing petrol and diesel from regular retail fuel stations. These consumers will now have to source fuel through dedicated bulk supply channels.

title: Complaints filed over alleged mangrove destruction for STP near Sarovaram
content: The complaint states that there is clear evidence that trees and vegetation were uprooted using a JCB and that the area was subsequently filled with soil

title: West Bengal police breaks into Abhishek Banerjee’s residence in early hours of June 13
content: Personnel from Salboni police station in the State’s Paschim Medinipur district entered the Trinamool leader’s residence and said that they were looking for Sumit Roy, the personnel assistant of Abhishek Banerjee

title: Murari Babu, former TDB official accused in Sabarimala gold theft case, passes away
content: Murari Babu has been ailing for some time, and the death occurred around 12.48 a.m., said authorities of the Amrita Hospital in Kochi

title: AI sovereignty hawks see red as U.S. moves to block Anthropic’s Mythos and Fable models
content: While training Large Language Models is a prohibitively expensive undertaking, national security commentators have long warned of over-reliance on foreign technology in critical fields like AI

title: Road to be renamed after Major Mukund Varadarajan
content: The martyr was posthumously awarded the Ashoka Chakra. The Sivakarthikeyan-starrer hit Tamil film Amaran that was released in 2024 was based on his life.

title: Operation Sindoor demonstrated IAF capability to destroy enemy infrastructure with accuracy and precision: Defence Minister
content: Rajnath Singh highlights growing role of women as first batch of  National Defence Academy women officers get wings at AFA Dundigal

title: The human cost of a deadly weed killer: banning of  paraquat in Telangana
content: In March, Telangana banned paraquat, one of the world’s most widely used and highly toxic weedicides, becoming only the third State in India to do so. The herbicide is responsible for hundreds of deaths each year, most of them by taking their own lives, and some accidental. Serish Nanisetti reports on the campaign that led to the ban and why much more remains to be done

title: ‘Amaran’ effect: How a romantic scene is causing trouble for this Chennai student
content: He claims that the crumpled paper that Sai Pallavi tosses at Sivakarthikeyan in a scene has a phone number resembling his

title: Will voice Global South’s aspirations in G7 summit, says PM Modi ahead of trip to France, Slovakia
content: The Prime Minister said  “I am confident that my visits to France and the Slovak Republic will reinforce India’s deepening engagement with both Europe and the G7”

title: Film based on women’s rights activist wins a short film award
content: Selected from over 12,000 entries across India, including nearly 7,000 Hindi films, Riha received the award from filmmaker and jury member Anurag Kashyap, who called it “literally a perfect 10”

title: Punjab may go to polls in November, Mann to return as CM: Kejriwal
content: “I have been told that the elections will be held in November [2026], not February [2027]. Only four months are left. Now we all have only one task — to make Bhagwant Singh Mann Chief Minister again,” said the ruling party’s national convener.

title: Andhra Premier League 2026: IPL-style thrills with high scores and close finishes
content: High scores and nail-biting finishes are bringing IPL-like excitement to the fifth season of the Andhra Premier League

title: Young activist’s 7,000-km walk highlights climate and animal welfare issues
content: Climate and animal welfare activist Ayan Banerjee is walking 7,000 kilometres 7,000 kilometres across India’s coastline, gathering carrying stories of environmental distress, kindness and grassroots action

title: Maharashtra to table ‘Women Farmers Empowerment Bill’ during upcoming Monsoon Session
content: Mr. Fadnavis reviewed a preliminary presentation on the draft Bill at a meeting attended by Deputy Chief Minister Sunetra Ajit Pawar, Agriculture Minister Dattatray Bharane and Minister of State for Agriculture Ashish Jaiswal at his official residence on Friday

title: Odisha CM announces free education from KG to PG in govt. institutions
content: The State launches Chief Minister Annapurna Yojana, under which all beneficiaries of the National Food Security Act and State Food Security Scheme will receive, an additional 5 kg of rice per month free of cost

title: Chest-deep in floodwater, Cyberabad constable pulls woman to safety from submerged car
content: Police said the flood water pressure prevented the woman from opening the car doors from inside, trapping her as the water level rose

title: Centre denies reports of fourth vessel attacked near Oman; says all crew members safe
content: MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal denied reports that MT Liaki Freedom was attacked on Friday night; he said the Ministry contacted the vessel and confirmed that all crew were safe

title: No formal proposal yet, say senior NCP(SP) leaders amid buzz over merger with Congress
content: While the Congress’s Maharashtra unit has refused comment on speculations about the merger, sources said a proposal to this effect has been in the pipeline for some time.

title: Auto driver’s daughter bags three gold medals in Bachelor of Physiotherapy
content: Spandhana I., daughter of an auto driver, achieved remarkable academic success by graduating with three gold medals in Bachelor of Physiotherapy from Padmashree Institute of Physiotherapy in Bengaluru this year.

title: Will bring Tatas to Bengal but land at Singur no longer belongs to govt., says Bengal CM
content: The Chief Minister who has been in office for about five weeks says that benefits of the double-engine government are slowly reaching the people of West Bengal

title: Supreme Court dismisses Meenakshi Natarajan’s plea against rejection of Rajya Sabha candidature
content: Ms. Natarajan’s counsel apprised the top court that the rejection of her candidature suffered from a ‘patent error’ and warranted judicial intervention

title: Congress launches nationwide campaign on paper leaks, youth unemployment
content: Leader of the Opposition in the Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi will be addressing student convention on June 17

title: Sun and sand: Bapatla beckons tourists with beach tourism
content: With Andhra Pradesh witnessing renewed focus on coastal development, Bapatla district is positioning itself as a major beach tourism hub; at the heart of the transformation lies Suryalanka Beach, which was once known for weekend visitors, now attracting tourists with its improved connectivity, upgraded safety systems, and serene environs

title: From Kerala to latex: Tracing London-based designer Harri KS’s unlikely rise
content: Raised around rubber plantations in Kerala, designer Harri KS is rethinking latex, form, and the idea of wearability

title: ‘Deep-seated’ bias for male child persists despite improving sex ratio, says Supreme Court
content: The top court refused to quash criminal proceedings against a Maharashtra doctor under the Pre-Conception and Pre-Natal Diagnostic Techniques (Prohibition of Sex Selection) Act, 1994

title: No new Nipah cases in Kozhikode; three hospitalised for observation
content: Ten more people added to the updated contact list, taking the total number of existing contacts under surveillance to 87 on Friday. Of them, four were categorised as highest-risk contacts, 16 as high-risk and 67 as low-risk contacts.

title: Flight headed to Kozhikode makes emergency landing at Kochi airport
content: As per established safety protocols, a local standby was declared, followed by a full emergency at 3.34 a.m. to ensure the readiness of all airport emergency response services

title: NLC emerges as preferred bidder for a critical mineral block in Telangana
content: The announcement comes close on the heels of NLC signing an MoU with CSIR-Central Electrochemical Research Institute (CSIR-CECRI), Karaikudi, to foster collaboration in the field of critical and strategic minerals beneficiation and extraction technologies

title: Telangana sees tepid monsoon onset, no strong systems in sight
content: While there may be occasional bursts of heavy rain, most rainfall in the coming days will likely be light to moderate, according to meteorologists at the Telangana Development Planning Society

title: BJP behaving like North Korean leader Kim Jong Un: Revanth Reddy
content: After illegally snatching Meenakshi Natarajan’s seat, the BJP and BRS were indulging in a misinformation campaign  like allegations of ‘coverts’ within the Congress to only divert attention

title: Minimum one Sanskrit batch required for Classes 6 and 9 in Kendriya Vidyalayas
content: Keeping Sanskrit as an option in all KVs ensures a smooth transition for students facing frequent transfers, letting others choose regional languages; some KVs face challenges of limited teachers and resources

title: Jaishankar speaks to Rubio, lodges strong protest over U.S. Navy attacks that killed three Indians
content: “Spoke to U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio this evening. I reiterated India’s strong protest at the attacks by the U.S. Navy in the Gulf that killed three Indian mariners,” Mr. Jaishankar said.

title: AI-171 crash: AAIB releases interim statement on probe
content: Final report to be released after all investigative work has been completed, and the mandatory international review and consultation processes concluded, says Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau

title: Congress does not require neutrality of the Indian State to function: Rahul Gandhi
content: In a speech that outlined the road ahead for the INDIA bloc, the Leader of the Opposition in the Lok Sabha asserted Congress’s primacy, acknowledged constraints, and urged unity amid sharp internal differences

title: Regional parties should rejoin Congress, strengthen Opposition unity: Ashok Gehlot
content: The former Chief Minister said safeguarding democratic values should be the foremost priority for all political forces, as the democracy in the country was “under serious threat”

title: Signature of rebels Trinamool MPs in public, many actor turned Parliamentarians switch sides
content: A majority of the rebel Trinamool Congress MPs are first time Parliamentarian, including cricketer turned politician Yusuf Pathan, actor Saayoni Ghosh and Cooch Behar MP Jagadish Verma Basunia among others

title: Supported by CJP, Lucknow students protest lack of transparency, irregularities in recruitment drives
content: CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke joins protest; protesters demand independent high-level inquiry into Lekhpal exam irregularities, seek permanent grievance redressal mechanism for competitive exam aspirants

title: Opinion: Opinion | The PoK Mess Exposes the Limits of Pakistan's Kashmir Narrative
content: Islamabad has long viewed political mobilisation in PoK through an external lens of alleged Indian 'interference'. But the recent uprisings in the region lays bare the limits of its own policy.

title: Hyderabad Techie, 37, Jumps From 6th Floor With Infant In Arms, Baby Survives
content: Police said the baby escaped a fatal fall after landing on a protective fence before hitting the ground. Residents rushed the infant to a nearby hospital, where the child is undergoing treatment.

title: Why Anthropic Disabled Its Most Powerful AI Model Days After Launch
content: Anthropic disabled public access to its most powerful AI model, Fable 5, just three days after its release.

title: From Concerts To Bhajan Clubbing: Inside India's Rs 13,000-Crore Boom
content: According to a BookMyShow-EY Parthenon report, India's organised live entertainment market is already worth more than Rs 13,000 crore.

title: On Camera, Woman Government Staff Stabbed 8 Times In Neck, Back By Colleague In Maharashtra
content: The injured woman was initially taken to a rural hospital in Palghar. Due to the severity of her condition, she was later referred to another hospital for further treatment. Her condition is stated to be critical.

title: Hyderabad YouTuber Stabbed To Death At Home, In-Laws Among Suspects
content: A Hyderabad based YouTuber was stabbed to death inside his home in a suspected family dispute attack.

title: Madhur Virli's Rape 'Joke' Sparks Outrage, Adds To 'Rs 370 Ki Biryani' Row
content: A video from stand-up comedian Madhur Virli's 2024 show 'Love & Latex' has surfaced amid 'Rs 370 ki biryani' row and shows him making remarks about rape.

title: Anthropic Suspends Fable 5, Mythos 5 AI Models After US Security Order
content: Just three days after publicly launching Fable 5, the company said in a blog post that it received a government directive banning all foreign nationals from accessing Fable 5 and Mythos 5

title: Peace Deal Soon? US Official 80-85% Confident, But Iran Has Some Conditions
content: Though pitched as a major diplomatic victory, the proposed agreement will only establish a 60-day ceasefire that would be followed by another set of negotiations.

title: 3 Indians Died In US Strikes. 10 Others Were Killed When A Jet Fell From Sky
content: Three Indians were killed in an US strike on a vessel off the coast of Oman. It is not the first time that Indians have come under the line of fire from American miscalculations.

title: Glowing Orbs, Potato-like Object: In US' New UFO Files, No Answers On Aliens
content: A person whose name is blacked out reported coming home and seeing an intense light hovering below the trees in their backyard.

content: Tren de Aragua has been labeled by the United States as a terrorist organization. Guerrero Flores was chargedwith racketeering conspiracy and other crimes, including lending support to terrorists in crimes

title: From Homes To Fuel: What Elon Musk's $1 Trillion Can Actually Buy
content: Musk's new title arrives amid a wider acceleration for the richest of the rich. Year after year, his former (although now very distant) billionaires club has reaped a growing number of members - from tech titans to celebrities.

title: 1 Dead, 10 Injured In Texas Shooting, Suspect Fired At Cop Days Earlier
content: The suspect, 45-year-old Victor Mata Villarreal, already was being sought by authorities when he began firing at police and bystanders in Midland on Friday

title: Weather LIVE Updates: Delhi Records Coolest June Day So Far As Rain Brings Relief From Heat
content: Weather Updates Today, Heatwave, IMD Alert LIVE Updates: Weather conditions are expected to improve after the next two days.

title: 38 'close deals' later: Why has Trump been saying Iran war to end soon since March?
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. Join us on a journey across continents as we unravel the stories that shape our interconnected world. Read More

title: Buying property? The seller’s PAN could become your tax problem
content: Lubna Kably is a senior editor, who focuses on various policies and legislation. In particular, she writes extensively on immigration and tax policies. The Indian diaspora is the largest in the world; through her articles she demystifies the immigration-policy related developments in select countries for outbound students, job aspirants and employees. She also analyses the impact of Income-tax and GST related developments for individuals and business entities. Read More

title: US missiles hit ships with Indian crew. Now, Trump blames Iran
